PhD Student in Computational Mass Spectrometry

We are seeking a PhD student to develop novel algorithms for the analysis of tandem mass spectrometry data. The ideal candidate has a background in computer science, bioinformatics, or a related field, and a strong interest in machine learning and data-driven science.

**Requirements**

- MSc degree in Computer Science, Bioinformatics, or related field
- Strong programming skills (Python preferred)
- Interest in machine learning and computational biology
- Good written and oral communication skills in English

In machine learning, deep learning (DL) focuses on utilizing multilayered neural networks to perform tasks such as classification, regression, and representation learning.

In machine learning, deep learning (DL) focuses on utilizing multilayered neural networks to perform tasks such as classification, regression, and representation learning. The field takes inspiration from biological neuroscience and revolves around stacking artificial neurons into layers and "training" them to process data. The adjective "deep" refers to the use of multiple layers (ranging from three to several hundred or thousands) in the network. Methods used can be supervised, semi-supervised or unsupervised.[2]

In machine learning, deep learning (DL) focuses on utilizing multilayered neural networks to perform tasks such as classification, regression, and representation learning. The field takes inspiration from biological neuroscience and revolves around stacking artificial neurons into layers and “training” them to process data. The adjective “deep” refers to the use of multiple layers (ranging from three to several hundred or thousands) in the network. Methods used can be supervised, semi-supervised or unsupervised.[2]

Machine learning (ML) is a field of study in artificial intelligence concerned with the development and study of statistical algorithms that can learn from data and generalize to unseen data, and thus perform tasks without explicit instructions.

Machine learning (ML) is a field of study in artificial intelligence concerned with the development and study of statistical algorithms that can learn from data and generalize to unseen data, and thus perform tasks without explicit instructions.[1] Within a subdiscipline of machine learning, advances in the field of deep learning have allowed neural networks, a class of statistical algorithms, to surpass many previous machine learning approaches in performance.

Machine learning (ML) is a field of study in artificial intelligence concerned with the development and study of statistical algorithms that can learn from data and generalize to unseen data, and thus perform tasks without explicit instructions.[1] Within a subdiscipline of machine learning, advances in the field of deep learning have allowed neural networks, a class of statistical algorithms, to surpass many previous machine learning approaches in performance.
Deep Learning for Peptide Identification from Tandem Mass Spectra
Develop and evaluate a deep learning model to improve de novo peptide sequencing from tandem mass spectrometry data. The project combines neural network design with large public spectral datasets.
Develop and evaluate a deep learning model to improve de novo peptide sequencing from tandem mass spectrometry data. The project combines neural network design with large public spectral datasets.
Graph Neural Networks for Spectral Library Matching
Explore graph neural network architectures to improve spectral library matching in proteomics workflows, leveraging structural relationships between fragmentation spectra.
Explore graph neural network architectures to improve spectral library matching in proteomics workflows, leveraging structural relationships between fragmentation spectra.
Benchmarking Metabolomics Tools for Non-Targeted Analysis
Design a systematic benchmark study comparing state-of-the-art computational tools for untargeted metabolomics. Includes developing evaluation criteria and applying tools to reference datasets.
